risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance risks are rated as Substantial, indicating significant fragility in the institutional environment that could impede project implementation and sustainability of recovery efforts.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance [Risk Category] Substantial [Rating at Approval and Last Approved Rating]

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints for Recovery Management
risk_summary: Institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability is rated as High risk, suggesting the implementing agency (SEDECOAS) and government structures may struggle to effectively manage the recovery and reconstruction program, particularly in fragile governance contexts.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability [Risk Category] High [Rating at Approval and Last Approved Rating]

risk_title: Data Gaps on Vulnerable Population Inclusion in Recovery
risk_summary: Targets for Indigenous Peoples (IPs) and Afro-descendants (ADs) participation remain undefined (TBD) due to unavailability of final impact assessments, creating risk that vulnerable populations affected by cyclone disaster and potential conflict dynamics may be excluded from recovery benefits.
evidence_quote: Percentage of which are IPs and ADs (Text)... Given the unavailability of final impact assessment and data, this target will be agreed upon with the GoH once the assessments are completed.
